title: potentilla glandulosa subsp. nevadensis
intro: Potentilla glandulosa subsp. nevadensis, also known as Silver Buffaloberry or simply Buffaloberry, is a shrub species native to the mountainous regions of western North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, containing a high concentration of vitamins A and C, as well as other nutrients like potassium and iron, which provide various health benefits, such as improving immune function and reducing inflammation. The berries of the Buffaloberry plant can be prepared into herbal teas, jams, or dried to be used as a dietary supplement, offering a natural source of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. Additionally, the plant's leaves and stems can be used to create a medicinal tincture or infusion, further showcasing its potential as a natural remedy for various health issues.
